name: Paseo de los Molinos Trail
name_original: Paseo de los Molinos – ida y vuelta desde Folgoso de la Ribera
description: Enjoy an easy 3.3-mile (5.2 km) hike on the Paseo de los Molinos Trail, featuring historical mills and a scenic waterfall.
region_intro: Hiking around Folgoso De La Ribera offers diverse landscapes within the El Bierzo region of León, Spain. The area is characterized by the Boeza River and its riparian forests, featuring lush woodlands of oaks, alders, ash, and poplars. Trails often follow riverbanks, leading through varied terrain that includes historic mills and scenic mountain slopes. Best hiking trails around Folgoso De La Ribera The most popular hiking route is Paseo de los Molinos Trail, a 3.3 miles (5.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 26 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the Boeza River through lush forests and past historic mills. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cañones del Górgora Trail, an easy 3.6 miles (5.8 km) path. This trail explores a scenic canyon with varied natural features. Local hikers also love the Embalse de Bembibre, Pozo de las Hoyas y Matavenero – desde San Facundo, a 6.8 miles (10.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain including a reservoir and historic villages, often completed in about 3 hours 12 minutes. Hiking around Folgoso De La Ribera is defined by riverbanks, dense forests, and historic mills, offering a mix of natural beauty and cultural heritage. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside strolls to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Folgoso De La Ribera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from around 40 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Folgoso De La Ribera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Folgoso de la Ribera?
A: There are over 10 hiking trails around Folgoso de la Ribera, offering a variety of experiences from easy riverside strolls to more challenging mountain ascents. The komoot community has explored these routes over 200 times.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on hikes in Folgoso de la Ribera?
A: Hiking in Folgoso de la Ribera often features the serene Boeza River and its lush riparian forests, filled with oaks, alders, ash, and poplars. Many trails lead past historic mills and offer views of picturesque waterfalls, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5542980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de las Hoyas Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gualta Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter unique natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sulphur Spring&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, Folgoso de la Ribera offers several easy routes suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050764726/paseo-de-los-molinos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo de los Molinos Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a great option, following the Boeza River through forests and past historic mills. Another easy choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047153647/canones-del-gorgora-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañones del Górgora Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which explores a scenic canyon.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Folgoso de la Ribera?
A: The trails in Folgoso de la Ribera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from nearly 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the soothing presence of the Boeza River, and the blend of natural beauty with historical elements like the old mills.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for more experienced hikers, there are several challenging routes. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041030422/pico-catoute-and-pico-de-la-cernella-circular-via-the-laguna-de-la-rebeza-from-colinas-del-campo-de-martin-moro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Catoute and Pico de la Cernella – circular via the Laguna de la Rebeza from Colinas del Campo de Martín Moro&lt;/a&gt;, which involves significant elevation gain and offers expansive views. Another difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041157181/laguna-de-la-rebeza-y-campo-de-santiago-circular-desde-colinas-de-campo-reserva-de-la-biosfera-valles-de-omana-y-luna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Rebeza y Campo de Santiago – circular desde Colinas de Campo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Folgoso de la Ribera?
A: Yes, many of the hiking routes in Folgoso de la Ribera are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041030422/pico-catoute-and-pico-de-la-cernella-circular-via-the-laguna-de-la-rebeza-from-colinas-del-campo-de-martin-moro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Catoute and Pico de la Cernella&lt;/a&gt; route is a circular option, as is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041157181/laguna-de-la-rebeza-y-campo-de-santiago-circular-desde-colinas de campo-reserva-de-la-biosfera-valles-de-omana-y-luna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Rebeza y Campo de Santiago&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest along the trails?
A: Beyond natural beauty, the region is rich in history. The "Ruta de los Molinos" (Mill Route) features several restored historic mills powered by the Boeza River. The area also includes historical routes, such as variants of the Camino de Santiago, adding a cultural dimension to your hike. You can also explore the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5542985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matavenero Eco Village&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the terrain like on the hiking trails?
A: The terrain around Folgoso de la Ribera is quite varied. You'll find well-maintained paths along riverbanks, through dense forests of oaks and chestnuts, and up mountain slopes. Some municipal routes connect different villages, offering a mix of landscapes, though some sections may be narrow and require caution.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or attractions to look out for?
A: Absolutely! Besides the waterfalls, you can discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5542985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matavenero Eco Village&lt;/a&gt;, a unique settlement. Keep an eye out for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5542974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wooden Bridge over the Ríal Stream&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1657319" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Folgoso Dam&lt;/a&gt;, which is near the fluvial beach of Folgoso de la Ribera.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Folgoso de la Ribera?
A: The diverse landscapes of Folgoso de la Ribera make it appealing for hiking across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with lush greenery in spring and rich colors in autumn. Summer is also popular, especially for trails along the Boeza River where you can enjoy the natural fluvial beach after your hike.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by a reservoir?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047117229/embalse-de-bembibre-pozo-de-las-hoyas-y-matavenero-desde-san-facundo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Bembibre, Pozo de las Hoyas y Matavenero – desde San Facundo&lt;/a&gt; trail leads through varied terrain, including a reservoir, offering scenic views of the water and surrounding landscapes.
tips: A good time to take a breather on our route through the Paseo de los Molinos. It is important to take the detour to the right and not cross the bridge that we will see.

A beautiful waterfall that we found during our route along the Paseo de los Molinos. This is where the Paseo de los Molinos ends, having to return along the same path. However, we can decide to approach the Light Factory by a slightly more demanding path.
Nice walk for all audiences. We will walk next to the sound of the water and under a leafy forest while we get to know the mills of the place.

faqs.question: Where does the Paseo de los Molinos Trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The Paseo de los Molinos Trail is a round trip, meaning it starts and ends in the same location. While specific parking details aren't provided, trails of this nature often have designated parking areas near the trailhead, especially if they are popular. It's advisable to check local maps or Komoot's tour details for the precise starting point in Folgoso de la Ribera.

faqs.question: Is the Paseo de los Molinos Trail a loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Paseo de los Molinos Trail is designed as a round trip, meaning it's a loop trail. You will start and finish your hike in the same location, offering a continuous and varied experience without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is rated as 'easy' and is suitable for beginners and families. With a distance of just over 5 km and minimal elevation gain, it offers a pleasant and accessible hiking experience. You'll find features like a Picnic Area on Paseo de los Molinos, making it ideal for a leisurely outing.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Paseo de los Molinos Trail?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can expect to see several interesting features. The trail is known for its historical mills, giving it its name. You'll also encounter a beautiful Waterfall of the Paseo de los Molinos and enjoy the scenic Mill Walk itself, which often follows riverbeds and showcases local flora.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Based on the general characteristics of 'Paseo de los Molinos' trails, spring and autumn are often ideal. Spring brings vibrant blooms and pleasant temperatures, while autumn offers beautiful foliage. Summer can be warm, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if hiking during that season. Winter might be cooler but can also offer unique scenery.

faqs.question: Is the Paseo de los Molinos Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this particular 'Paseo de los Molinos' trail are not detailed, many similar trails in Spain are generally dog-friendly, especially if they are rural and easy. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially near any livestock or in areas with other hikers, and to carry waste bags to clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, typically, trails like the Paseo de los Molinos, which are public hiking routes, do not require permits or charge entrance fees. You should be able to access and enjoy the trail freely.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain should I expect on the hike?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally easy, suggesting well-maintained paths. You can expect varied landscapes, potentially following riverbeds or traversing gentle hillsides. Given the 'Mill Walk' highlight, sections will likely be near water sources and feature riparian vegetation. The overall terrain is suitable for casual hikers.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restrooms or cafes along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail features a dedicated Picnic Area on Paseo de los Molinos, which is a great spot for a break. While specific information about restrooms or cafes directly on the trail isn't available, the trail starts from Folgoso de la Ribera, a village where you might find local amenities before or after your hike.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on the Paseo de los Molinos Trail?
faqs.answer: For this easy, 5 km hike, comfortable walking shoes are essential. We recommend bringing water, especially on warmer days, and snacks. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is also advisable. If you plan to use the picnic area, consider packing a lunch. Don't forget your camera to capture the waterfall and scenic views!

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the trail?
faqs.answer: The Paseo de los Molinos Trail is approximately 5.25 km long and takes about 1 hour and 26 minutes to complete at a moderate hiking pace. This duration can vary depending on your speed and how often you stop to enjoy the sights, like the waterfall or the picnic area.
